reasoning: Initial state shows A=10, B=5, Y=15. I need to determine how Y relates to A and B. I will increase A to see if Y changes, which will help identify the relationship.
Initial state shows A=10, B=5, Y=15. I need to determine how Y relates to A and B. I will increase A to see if Y changes, which will help identify the relationship.

From the history, increasing A from 10 to 20 (delta +10) increased Y from 15 to 25 (delta +10). Increasing A from 20 to 30 (delta +10) increased Y from 25 to 35 (delta +10). This indicates Y = A + C. At A=10, Y=15 implies C=5. At A=30, Y=35 implies C=5. The constant 5 matches the value of B. Thus, the relationship is likely Y = A + B. I will verify this by changing B.

From the history, increasing A by 10 increased Y by 10, suggesting a coefficient of 1 for A. Increasing B from 5 to 10 (delta +5) increased Y from 35 to 40 (delta +5), suggesting a coefficient of 1 for B. The data points (10,5,15), (20,5,25), (30,5,35), and (30,10,40) all fit the linear equation Y = A + B. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the relationship.
